ARG.name: Argentina
ARG.intro: Three-time global champions La Albiceleste enter the tournament looking to defend their title with their trademark La Pausa style of play—a rhythmic, possession-based approach driven by the genius of Lionel Messi, Alexis Mac Allister, and Julián Álvarez. Placed in Group J alongside Algeria, Austria, and Jordan, the team will be backed by their famously passionate fan group, La Hinchada, as they look to build on a rich tournament history that spans six final appearances and decades of footballing dominance.

NOR.name: Norway
NOR.intro: Returning to the world stage after a 26-year drought to build on their modest history of two previous Round of 16 appearances, *Landslaget* brings a lethal, direct, and high-pressing style of play to a grueling Group I alongside heavyweights France, Senegal, and Iraq. Driven by the devastating attacking tandem of Erling Haaland and Martin Ødegaard, the Red, White, and Blue will be fueled by an influx of passionate, Viking-hat-clad supporters injecting brilliant energy into North American stadiums as they hunt for a deep tournament run.

AUS.name: Australia
AUS.intro: Making their seventh tournament appearance and looking to replicate their historic 2006 and 2022 Round of 16 breakthroughs, the *Socceroos* bring a highly resilient, physical, and defensively disciplined style of play to a challenging Group F alongside the United States, Türkiye, and Paraguay. Relying on the rock-solid veteran experience of captain Mathew Ryan and towering defender Harry Souttar alongside the explosive flair of teenage winger Nestory Irankunda, Australia will be fiercely backed by the roaring chants of their famously dedicated fan group, the *Green and Gold Army*.

BRA.name: Brazil
BRA.intro: The only nation to have featured in every single tournament iteration, record five-time champions A Seleção enter Group C alongside Morocco, Scotland, and Haiti determined to break a 24-year title drought by replicating their historic 1994 triumph on North American soil. Defined by their world-famous Joga Bonito philosophy of expressive, attacking flair, Brazil is spearheaded by the explosive brilliance of Vinícius Júnior and a heralded return for Neymar, all while backed by the sea of yellow jerseys and rhythmic samba drums of their passionate Movimento Verde e Amarelo fan base.

CAN.name: Canada
CAN.intro: Co-hosting the tournament for the first time in their history, *Les Rouges* look to secure their first-ever tournament win after falling short in 1986 and 2022, battling through Group B alongside Switzerland, Qatar, and Bosnia and Herzegovina. Boasting an aggressive, high-pressing, and transitional style of play, the Canadian squad relies on the explosive pace of global icon Alphonso Davies and the clinical finishing of Jonathan David, all while playing in front of a sea of red and the booming chants of their official supporters' group, *The Voyageurs*.

COL.name: Colombia
COL.intro: Returning to the global stage for their seventh appearance after missing out in 2022, Los Cafeteros bring an electrifying, high-tempo, and physically aggressive style of play to Group K alongside Portugal, DR Congo, and Uzbekistan. Headlined by the timeless playmaker James Rodríguez and the explosive world-class threat of Luis Díaz, Colombia's campaign to best their historic 2014 quarterfinal finish will be propelled by the deafening chants and sea of yellow shirts supplied by La Fiebre Amarilla, their notoriously vibrant fan group.

ENG.name: England
ENG.intro: Seeking to replicate the historic triumph of their legendary 1966 squad, the Three Lions bring a highly technical, possession-heavy style of play to Group G alongside Croatia, Ghana, and Panama. Spearheaded by the world-class clinical finishing of Harry Kane and the creative brilliance of Jude Bellingham and Bukayo Saka, England will be aggressively backed by the thunderous, pints-in-the-air anthems of their famously traveling fan base as they look to transition from perennial contenders to global champions.

FRA.name: France
FRA.intro: Two-time champions Les Bleus land in Group I alongside Senegal, Iraq, and Norway, eager to build on a storied history that includes back-to-back final appearances in 2018 and 2022. Boasting a highly technical, fast-breaking, and devastatingly clinical style of play, the French squad is headlined by the world-class supremacy of Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé, all while backed by the roaring chants and traveling tricolor flags of Les Irrésistibles Français.

GER.name: Germany
GER.intro: Four-time global champions Die Mannschaft enter Group E alongside Ecuador, Ivory Coast, and Curaçao, highly motivated to reassert their dominance and build on a legendary tournament history that features eight total final appearances. Flaunting a high-octane, possession-oriented, and tactically fluid style of play, the German squad is spearheaded by the generational brilliance of Florian Wirtz and Jamal Musiala alongside the clinical edge of Kai Havertz, heavily backed by the passionate chants of their official fan group, the Fan Club Nationalmannschaft.

HAI.name: Haiti
HAI.intro: Ending a 52-year tournament absence to make their historic second appearance on the global stage, *Les Grenadiers* bring an emotional, highly resilient, and swift transitional style of play to a demanding Group C alongside Brazil, Morocco, and Scotland. Powered by the attacking threat of legendary top scorer Duckens Nazon, Wilson Isidor, and midfielder Danley Jean Jacques, the Red and Blue will be lifted by a proud, passionate Haitian diaspora filling North American stadiums with rhythmic, carnival-like celebration as they hunt for their nation’s first-ever tournament points.

JPN.name: Japan
JPN.intro: Making their eighth consecutive appearance and desperately striving to finally shatter their Round of 16 ceiling, the *Samurai Blue* bring a highly disciplined, lightning-fast transitional, and tactically fluid style of play to a fiercely competitive Group F alongside the Netherlands, Sweden, and Tunisia. Armed with top-tier European pedigree in Takehiro Tomiyasu, Wataru Endō, and the creative wizardry of Takefusa Kubo, Japan's quest for a historic deep run will be loudly powered by the ultra-coordinated drumming, synchronized chanting, and immaculate stadium-cleaning traditions of their world-renowned *Ultra Nippon* supporters.

KOR.name: South Korea
KOR.intro: Boasting an impressive streak of 11 consecutive tournament appearances, the *Taegeuk Warriors* bring a relentless, high-stamina, and lightning-fast counter-attacking style of play to an intriguing Group A alongside co-hosts Mexico, South Africa, and Czechia. Anchored by the world-class attacking spark of Son Heung-min, the creative midfield vision of Lee Kang-in, and the defensive muscle of Kim Min-jae, South Korea will look to channel their legendary 2002 semifinal heroics while backed by the thunderous, synchronized drumming of their iconic *Red Devils* supporters' group.

MAR.name: Morocco
MAR.intro: Looking to build on their legendary, historic 2022 campaign where they became the first African nation to ever reach the semifinals, the *Atlas Lions* bring a rigid defensive structure combined with electric, technical counter-attacking to Group C alongside Brazil, Scotland, and Haiti. Spearheaded by world-class full-back Achraf Hakimi and the attacking flair of Brahim Díaz and Hakim Ziyech, Morocco will be heavily backed by their fiercely loyal fan group, the *Moroccan Ultras*, who are set to bring a deafening, crimson-colored wall of passion to North American stadiums.

NED.name: Netherlands
NED.intro: Known as the perpetual bridesmaids of the tournament with three historic runner-up finishes, *Oranje* enters Group F alongside Japan, Sweden, and Tunisia determined to finally capture an elusive first star. Driven by a fluid, possession-heavy style of play that mirrors their legendary Total Football roots, the Dutch squad leans on the world-class defensive stewardship of Virgil van Dijk alongside the creative spark of Frenkie de Jong, all while backed by the boisterous, brass-band-led dancing of their iconic *Oranje Legioen* fan base.

NZL.name: New Zealand
NZL.intro: Making their third historical appearance and hunting for their first-ever tournament victory after memorable group stage exits in 1982 and 2010, the *All Whites* bring a highly physical, structured, and direct counter-attacking style of play to a challenging Group G alongside Belgium, Egypt, and Iran. Spearheaded by the aerial dominance of Premier League striker Chris Wood and the emerging European talent of full-back Liberato Cacace, New Zealand will be backed by the proud, dedicated traveling contingent known as the *Flying Kiwis* as they seek a historic breakthrough into the knockout rounds.

PAN.name: Panama
PAN.intro: Making their historic second tournament appearance after debuting in 2018, Los Canaleros bring a highly energetic, physical, and counter-attacking style of play to a daunting Group L alongside England, Croatia, and Ghana. Fueled by the defensive grit of Michael Amir Murillo and the dynamic midfield creativity of Adalberto Carrasquilla, Panama will look to claim their first-ever knockout berth while backed by the thunderous, crimson-clad passion of their fan base, La Marea Roja.

POR.name: Portugal
POR.intro: Chasing their first-ever title to build on a proud tournament history highlighted by a third-place finish in 1966, *A Seleção das Quinas* brings a highly technical, possession-oriented, and fluid attacking style of play to Group K alongside Colombia, DR Congo, and debutants Uzbekistan. Driven by the creative midfield genius of Bruno Fernandes and Bernardo Silva alongside the explosive pace of Rafael Leão, the squad features legendary captain Cristiano Ronaldo embarking on a historic sixth tournament, fiercely backed by the thunderous chants and waving scarves of their dedicated *Seleção de Portugal* fan base.

RSA.name: South Africa
RSA.intro: Making their fourth tournament appearance and aiming to advance past the group stage for the first time in their history, *Bafana Bafana* bring a fast-paced, joyful, and fluid style of play known as *Diski* to a formidable Group A alongside co-hosts Mexico, South Korea, and Czechia. Armed with the attacking flair of Burnley striker Lyle Foster, the creative midfield veteran Themba Zwane, and the safe hands of captain Ronwen Williams, South Africa will be passionately backed by their yellow-and-green-clad fans who are ready to inject incredible energy and rhythmic celebration into North American stadiums.

ESP.name: Spain
ESP.intro: Boasting a rich tournament history highlighted by their iconic 2010 triumph, *La Roja* enters Group H alongside Uruguay, Saudi Arabia, and tournament debutants Cape Verde. Moving away from the classic, patient *Tiki-Taka* blueprint toward a high-stamina, vertically explosive style of play, the Spanish squad is fueled by the generational flair of Lamine Yamal and Nico Williams, fiercely backed by the passionate, traveling wall of support from the *Marea Roja* fan group.

SWE.name: Sweden
SWE.intro: Making their 13th appearance on the global stage, *Blågult* looks to replicate the magic of their historic 1958 runner-up finish as they navigate a highly competitive Group F alongside the Netherlands, Japan, and Tunisia. Boasting a physically imposing, direct, and devastatingly lethal attacking style of play, the Swedish side relies on the elite, world-class scoring prowess of Viktor Gyökeres and Alexander Isak, all while being fueled by a vibrant, traveling wall of yellow and the synchronized chants of their official supporters' group, *Camp Sweden*.

SUI.name: Switzerland
SUI.intro: Competing in their sixth consecutive tournament while looking to finally break past a historical quarterfinal ceiling they last reached in 1954, *Rossocrociati* face a dynamic Group B alongside co-hosts Canada, Qatar, and Bosnia and Herzegovina. Relying on a highly structured, defensively solid, and disciplined possession style of play, the Swiss squad is anchored by the world-class shot-stopping of Gregor Kobel, the defensive mastery of Manuel Akanji, and the veteran midfield leadership of captain Granit Xhaka, all while propelled by the thunderous "Hopp Schwiiz" chants of their dedicated traveling *Nati* fans.

USA.name: United States
USA.intro: Embracing the immense pressure of co-hosting the tournament on home soil, the *Stars and Stripes* are set to navigate a highly competitive Group D alongside Paraguay, Australia, and Türkiye as they chase the ultimate dream of besting their historic, nearly century-old third-place finish from 1930. Flaunting an energetic, high-intensity, and vertically explosive style of play, the American squad relies heavily on the world-class dynamic creativity of Christian Pulisic, Weston McKennie, and Gio Reyna, all while propelled by the thunderous, relentless chanting of the *American Outlaws* filling up stadiums coast to coast.

ALG.name: Algeria
ALG.intro: Making their fifth historical appearance on the global stage, *Les Fennecs* bring a highly technical, possession-oriented, and tactically fluid style of play to an intriguing Group J alongside reigning champions Argentina, Austria, and debutants Jordan. Driven by the veteran flair of captain Riyad Mahrez and the explosive attacking threat of Amine Gouiri and Mohamed Amoura, Algeria's quest to surpass their historic 2014 Round of 16 finish will be fiercely powered by the passionate, green-and-white-clad wall of supporters belonging to the *El Khadra* fan base.

AUT.name: Austria
AUT.intro: Making their eighth appearance on the global stage and seeking to channel the magic of their historic third-place finish in 1954, *Das Team* faces a daunting task in Group J alongside reigning champions Argentina, Algeria, and debutants Jordan. Deploying a fierce, high-octane, and aggressive counter-pressing style of play, the Austrian squad leans heavily on the world-class versatility of David Alaba and the creative midfield orchestrations of Marcel Sabitzer, all while fueled by the roaring, traveling wall of support from *Unsere Burschen* fans.

BEL.name: Belgium
BEL.intro: Making their eighth appearance on the global stage and seeking to channel the magic of their historic third-place finish in 1954, *Das Team* faces a daunting task in Group J alongside reigning champions Argentina, Algeria, and debutants Jordan. Deploying a fierce, high-octane, and aggressive counter-pressing style of play, the Austrian squad leans heavily on the world-class versatility of David Alaba and the creative midfield orchestrations of Marcel Sabitzer, all while fueled by the roaring, traveling wall of support from *Unsere Burschen* fans.

BIH.name: Bosnia and Herzegovina
BIH.intro: Making their historic second tournament appearance after debuting in 2014, *Zmajevi* (The Dragons) bring a physical, hard-working, and direct style of play to a dynamic Group B alongside co-hosts Canada, Qatar, and Switzerland. Led by the ageless, legendary goalscoring prowess of captain Edin Džeko alongside Bundesliga forward Ermedin Demirović and rising winger Esmir Bajraktarević, the Bosnian squad will look to secure their first-ever knockout berth while fueled by the fiery, deafening chants of their fiercely loyal *BHFanaticos* supporters' group.

CIV.name: Côte d'Ivoire
CIV.intro: Returning to the global tournament for the first time since 2014, *Les Éléphants* look to shatter their group-stage ceiling as they navigate a highly competitive Group E alongside European giants Germany, Ecuador, and debutants Curaçao. Flaunting a physically dominant, fast-paced, and explosive wing-heavy style of play, the Ivorian squad leans heavily on the midfield steel of captain Franck Kessié and the electric creativity of Amad Diallo and Simon Adingra, all while backed by the thunderous percussion bands and rhythmic, elephant-mimicking roars of their famously colorful supporters.

COD.name: DR Congo
COD.intro: Ending their staggering 52-year tournament drought to make their first appearance since debuting as Zaire in 1974, *Les Léopards* bring a physically imposing, highly energetic, and explosive counter-attacking style of play to a challenging Group K alongside powerhouse Portugal, Colombia, and debutants Uzbekistan. Spearheaded by the defensive steel of captain Chancel Mbemba and Aaron Wan-Bissaka alongside the lethal Premier League attacking threat of Yoane Wissa, the Congolese side will chase their nation's first-ever tournament points while backed by the thunderous, brass-heavy percussion bands and rhythmic dancing of their fiercely passionate, blue-and-yellow-clad supporters.

CPV.name: Cabo Verde
CPV.intro: Making a monumental debut as they celebrate their first-ever qualification on the global stage, the *Tubarões Azuis* (Blue Sharks) bring a highly technical, defensively resilient, and fast-transitioning style of play to a challenging Group H alongside Spain, Uruguay, and Saudi Arabia. Spearheaded by the commanding presence of Villarreal defender Logan Costa, veteran winger Ryan Mendes, and goalkeeper Bruno Varela, the island nation will be backed by the vibrant, instrument-filled passion of their dedicated *Crioulos* fan base as they seek a historic underdog story in North America.

CRO.name: Croatia
CRO.intro: Sustaining an incredible tournament legacy that includes finishing as 2018 runners-up and third in both 1998 and 2022, the *Vatreni* (The Fiery Ones) bring a highly technical, possession-controlling, and patient midfield-heavy style of play to a grueling Group L alongside England, Ghana, and Panama. Driven by the legendary, timeless brilliance of 40-year-old captain Luka Modrić alongside Mateo Kovačić and defensive star Joško Gvardiol, Croatia's quest to once again punch above their weight will be passionately fueled by the deafening chants and sea of red-and-white checkered jerseys brought by their fiercely loyal *Uvijek Vjerni* supporters.

CUW.name: Curaçao
CUW.intro: Making history as the smallest nation to ever qualify for the tournament, the *Blue Wave* brings a fast-transitioning, highly technical, and defensively resilient style of play to their monumental global debut in a challenging Group E alongside Germany, Ecuador, and the Ivory Coast. Anchored by the veteran leadership of captain Leandro Bacuna and the explosive attacking flair of English-based wingers Tahith Chong and Sontje Hansen, Curaçao's historic underdog journey will be fiercely backed by the vibrant, instrument-filled passion of their dedicated *Crioulos* supporters.

CZE.name: Czechia
CZE.intro: Returning to the global stage for the first time in 20 years, *Nároďák* (The National Team) looks to channel their historic legacy—which includes two runner-up finishes as Czechoslovakia—as they compete in a demanding Group A alongside South Korea, South Africa, and co-hosts Mexico. Deploying a highly physical, organized, and tactically disciplined style of play that emphasizes resilient defending and swift transitions, the Czech squad is led by the defensive steel of captain Ladislav Krejčí and the tireless midfield engine of Tomáš Souček, fiercely backed by the passionate traveling chants of their dedicated *Vlajkonoši* supporters' group.

ECU.name: Ecuador
ECU.intro: Making their fifth appearance in the tournament and aiming to surpass their historic 2006 Round of 16 ceiling, Ecuador brings a fearless, defensive-first style of play to Group E alongside Germany, Ivory Coast, and Curaçao. Anchored by the elite midfield presence of Moisés Caicedo, a rock-solid backline featuring Piero Hincapié, and the timeless clinical edge of Enner Valencia, La Tri boasts a balanced, high-intensity roster built to neutralize heavyweights and dominate transition play.

EGY.name: Egypt
EGY.intro: Looking to transcend a challenging tournament history that has never seen them progress past the first round, *The Pharaohs* bring a disciplined, counter-attacking, and defensively rigid style of play to an intriguing Group G alongside Belgium, Iran, and New Zealand. Governed by the world-class scoring instinct of talismanic captain Mohamed Salah alongside the European-honed attacking talents of Omar Marmoush and Trézéguet, the Egyptian side will be intensely backed by the deafening drums and passionate roars of the *Ultras* and casual supporters alike, creating a vibrant sea of red across North American stadiums.

GHA.name: Ghana
GHA.intro: Making their fifth appearance on the global stage, the *Black Stars* aim to match or surpass their historic 2010 quarterfinal run as they navigate a grueling Group L alongside England, Croatia, and Panama. Flaunting a highly energetic, physical, and lightning-fast attacking style of play, the Ghanaian squad is driven by the dynamic midfield power of Thomas Partey, the lethal wing play of Mohammed Kudus, and the creative spark of Ernest Nuamah, all while powered by the relentless drumming, dancing, and brass bands of the fiercely passionate *Ghana National Supporters Union* (GNSU).

IRN.name: Iran
IRN.intro: Making their seventh appearance on the global stage and desperate to finally break through a historic group-stage ceiling, *Team Melli* brings a famously rigid, compact defensive structure and lethal counter-attacking style of play to an intriguing Group G alongside Belgium, Egypt, and New Zealand. Spearheaded by the elite, clinical striking partnership of Sardar Azmoun and Mehdi Taremi alongside the creative wings of Alireza Jahanbakhsh, the Iranian squad will be fueled by the booming, rhythmic drums and passionate chants of their massive diaspora and traveling supporters’ group, the *Yuzpalangan* (Cheetahs).

IRQ.name: Iraq
IRQ.intro: Making their historic second tournament appearance and first since 1986, the *Usood al-Rafidayn* (Lions of Mesopotamia) bring a highly passionate, physically resilient, and fast-countering style of play to a daunting Group I alongside heavyweights France, Senegal, and Norway. Spearheaded by the clinical goalscoring prowess of Aymen Hussein alongside the dynamic attacking threat of Ali Al-Hamadi and young talent Ali Jasim, the Iraqi squad will look to capture their first-ever tournament win while fiercely propelled by the roaring, green-clad wall of their devoted *Al-Moutawaajiz* fan groups traveling across North America.

JOR.name: Jordan
JOR.intro: Making a monumental debut as they celebrate their first-ever qualification on the global stage, *Al Nashama* (The Chivalrous Ones) bring a fearlessly aggressive, high-pressing, and lethal transition style of play to a daunting Group J alongside reigning champions Argentina, Algeria, and Austria. Powered by the spectacular attacking chemistry of their star-studded duo Mousa Al-Tamari and Yazan Al-Naimat, Jordan's historic underdog campaign will be relentlessly driven by the synchronized drumming, booming chants, and unyielding national pride of their fiercely loyal, traveling supporters.

KSA.name: Saudi Arabia
KSA.intro: Making their seventh appearance on the global stage, the *Green Falcons* aim to recapture the magic of their historic 1994 Round of 16 run as they head into a formidable Group H alongside Spain, Uruguay, and debutants Cape Verde. Deploying a highly technical, fast-paced, and fluid passing style of play, the Saudi squad relies on the brilliant talismanic creativity of captain Salem Al-Dawsari and the sharp attacking threat of Firas Al-Buraikan, all while fiercely backed by the thunderous drumming and beautifully synchronized chanting.

MEX.name: Mexico
MEX.intro: Embracing the profound pride of co-hosting the tournament, *El Tri* is set to navigate an intriguing Group A alongside South Korea, South Africa, and Czechia as they desperately look to surpass their historic, home-soil quarterfinal finishes from 1970 and 1986. Flaunting a highly energetic, fast-transitioning, and technically fluid style of play, the Mexican squad relies on the veteran leadership of captain Edson Álvarez, the clinical form of striker Raúl Jiménez, and the ageless instincts of legendary goalkeeper Guillermo Ochoa, all while backed by the earth-shattering, unified roar of their fiercely loyal *La Ola Verde* fans filling the iconic Estadio Azteca.

PAR.name: Paraguay
PAR.intro: Returning to the tournament for the first time since 2010 to make their tenth historical appearance, *La Albirroja* brings their famed *garra*—a fierce, compact, and physical defensive counter-attacking style of play—to a demanding Group D alongside co-hosts the United States, Australia, and Türkiye. The Paraguayan side will lean heavily on the defensive steel of captain Gustavo Gómez and the electric, creative attacking prowess of Julio Enciso and Miguel Almirón, all while fueled by the unyielding, passionate backing of their dedicated red-and-white-clad supporters chasing supporters as white-clad supporters as they look to replicate or surpass their historic 2010 quarterfinal finish.

QAT.name: Qatar
QAT.intro: Making their second consecutive tournament appearance after debuting as hosts in 2022, *Al-Annabi* (The Maroons) bring a highly technical, fluid, and possession-oriented passing style to a wide-open Group B alongside co-hosts Canada, Switzerland, and Bosnia and Herzegovina. Spearheaded by the brilliant creative flair of two-time AFC Player of the Year Akram Afif, lethal all-time leading goalscorer Almoez Ali, and legendary captain Hassan Al-Haydos, the reigning Asian champions will look to advance past the group stage for the first time while backed by the passionate, coordinated drumming of their dedicated Qatar National Teams Fans Club.

SCO.name: Scotland
SCO.intro: Returning to the global tournament for the first time since 1998 to make their ninth historical appearance, the *Tartan Army*—Scotland's famously passionate, kilt-clad traveling supporters' group—will bring their boisterous energy to an intriguing Group C alongside heavyweights Brazil, Morocco, and Haiti. Deploying a highly organized, industrious, and wing-back-reliant style of play, the Scottish squad will lean heavily on the elite midfield engines of captain Andrew Robertson, Scott McTominay, and John McGinn as they fight to advance past the group stage for the first time in their nation's history.

SEN.name: Senegal
SEN.intro: Chasing the magic of their historic 2002 quarterfinal run and looking to build on their 2022 knockout appearance, the *Lions of Teranga* bring a physically imposing, high-pressing, and blisteringly fast wing-heavy style of play to a stacked Group I alongside France, Norway, and Iraq. Spearheaded by the legendary, talismanic presence of Sadio Mané alongside defensive rock Kalidou Koulibaly and the explosive midfield energy of Pape Matar Sarr, Senegal's ambitious campaign will be driven by the non-stop, hypnotic drumming and vibrant, synchronized choreography of the fiercely passionate *12ème Gaïndé* supporters' group.

TUN.name: Tunisia
TUN.intro: Entering their seventh global tournament with the stubborn ambition to finally shatter their historic group-stage ceiling, the *Eagles of Carthage* bring a highly disciplined, defensively rigid, and counter-attacking style of play to a brutal Group F alongside the Netherlands, Japan, and Sweden. Relying on the defensive steel of full-back Ali Abdi, the midfield engine of Mohamed Ali Ben Romdhane, and the explosive creativity of winger Elias Achouri, the Tunisian squad will look to grind out historic results while fueled by the relentless drumming, roaring chants, and passionate energy of their massive, traveling diaspora fan base.

TUR.name: Türkiye
TUR.intro: Making their highly anticipated return to the global tournament for the first time since their sensational, third-place finish in 2002, *Bizim Çocuklar* (Our Boys) bring a fiercely aggressive, high-tempo, and technically gifted style of play to a challenging Group D alongside co-hosts the United States, Australia, and Paraguay. Driven by the electric creativity of Real Madrid starlet Arda Güler, Inter Milan midfield maestro Hakan Çalhanoğlu, and Juventus winger Kenan Yıldız, Turkey's quest to replicate their historic bronze-medal legacy will be backed by an incredibly loud, pyrotechnic-loving, and intensely passionate traveling contingent of the *Amigo* led Turkish fan groups.

URU.name: Uruguay
URU.intro: Steeped in an illustrious tournament history that boasts two iconic world titles from 1930 and 1950, *La Celeste* (The Sky Blue) enters a highly competitive Group H alongside Spain, Saudi Arabia, and Cape Verde. Driven by their legendary *garra charrúa*—a fiercely aggressive, high-pressing, and unrelenting style of play—the Uruguayan squad will rely on the world-class engine of Federico Valverde and the clinical attacking bite of Darwin Núñez, all while fueled by the deafening chants and unyielding passion of their traveling *Charrúas* faithful.

UZB.name: Uzbekistan
UZB.intro: Making history as the first Central Asian nation to ever qualify for the tournament, the *White Wolves* bring a highly physical, rigid, and "safety-first" defensive style of play to their monumental global debut in a daunting Group K alongside Portugal, Colombia, and DR Congo. Spearheaded by the aerial presence of star captain and all-time leading scorer Eldor Shomurodov alongside Manchester City’s breakout defender Abdukodir Khusanov, Uzbekistan's historic underdog campaign will be passionately fueled by the synchronized, roaring chants of their traveling contingent of devoted Uzbek fans.
